creator: david@wood2.org.uk
text:
It is always helpful to copy maintainers into a PR about a port. I 
didn't receive any email about this PR - I noticed it only when double 
checking for any problems before starting work on the upgrade to 2.1.4.


I'm really rather confused by this PR. I submitted ports/129996 on 28 
December 2008, a month and a half before this PR, which updated the port 
to 2.1.3. miwi committed my patch within a couple of hours.


Evaluating the patches when updating the port is rather important - 
FreeRADIUS can be a rather complex piece of software to build and it's 
function is security critical, making any patches are potentially 
security critical (as was seen in the Debian OpenSSL debacle). I've 
tried very hard since taking over maintainership of the FreeRADIUS ports 
to eliminate all unnecessary patches and to try to keep patches to the 
build system rather than the code itself.

In fact, there was a problem to overcome post 2.0.5, which took a while 
to understand. It was a regression in the autoconf 2.61->2.62 upgrade - 
see ports/129996 for details. Without the corresponding fix, rlm_python 
won't build, which is why the port was marked BROKEN.

There's other things that the patch misses - such as the discontinuation 
of the old SNMP support in FreeRADIUS 2.1.3.



This PR offers a possible enhancement: making rlm_perl and rlm_python 
optional. FreeRADIUS 2 does have unlang, but my understanding is that 
rlm_perl and rlm_python are regarded as core functionality for 
FreeRADIUS 2. The FreeRADIUS developers have both rlm_perl and 
rlm_python turned on as standard, and I do not believe that there's good 
reason to turn either off by default in the port.


I realise that embedded users may want the total space taken when 
installing FreeRADIUS to be little as possible, which is why I left the 
NOPERL knob from net/freeradius intact. That tears out the dependency on 
Perl, but does not attempt to deal with the behaviour of configure.in. 
If you want to use this, build the port with make -DWITH_NOPERL.

As the comments in the Makefile explain, FreeRADIUS depends on Perl if 
it's found on the build system irrespective of what arguments are passed 
to configure. A core piece of functionality (Simultaneous-Use) can fail 
to work if Perl isn't present. In fact, net-snmp should be present for 
this functionality as well - but it isn't needed at build time.

Because FreeRADIUS depends on Perl if it is present, NOPERL is an ugly 
hack. I have discussed with the FreeRADIUS developers the possibility of 
changing configure.in to make dependency on Perl optional, but without 
any response. The more I have to patch configure.in in the port, the 
more of a maintenance headache such patches become.

There seems little point having an option not to build rlm_perl whilst 
still having Perl on the system - it will save very little space. 
rlm_perl can be tricky to build, but I've not had any reports of it 
failing to build successfully via the port.


I have not yet received a request for making rlm_python - and hence the 
dependency on Python - optional. Is this something that the submitter or 
someone he knows would use? If so, would a knob suffice rather an option 
- bearing in mind that I believe rlm_python to be core functionality for 
FreeRADIUS 2, I'd rather reduce the opportunity for foot-shooting.


If a 'light' FreeRADIUS 2 build is wished for, maybe the answer is a 
slave port (rather like net/freeradius-mysql is a slave port of 
net/freeradius) for embedded users, so that there's a package available. 
However, I don't want to pollute the ports tree with unnecessary slave 
ports unless there's a good reason for it.

I am open to enhancement suggestions and, even better, working patches - 
but I feel that this PR should be closed as superfluous.


I will shortly be updating the net/freeradius2 port to 2.1.4.


Best wishes,




David (maintainer, net/freeradius2)
-- 
David Wood
david@wood2.org.uk
